mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations and improve corporate financing conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods following a rate decline, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals severe labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, which overwhelms monetary support and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% over 60-day horizons, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals stabilizing manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day returns in recovery regimes, with negative returns occurring in >60% of cases, or the mechanism flips direction during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside contracting real GDP indicate stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to delay easing, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns due to compressed profit margins and higher discount rates.
failure_condition: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL and falling GDPC1 does not predict negative returns, with positive 60-day returns exceeding 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which consistently generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in >65% of observations, or the rolling information coefficient remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.0 million units and then stabilize or rise, it signals the exhaustion of the housing downturn, prompting equity market repricing and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of periods meeting the trigger condition, or the signal loses predictive power when HOUST remains below 900 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP decline exceeding 1.0% indicates severe economic contraction that triggers widespread corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: GDPC1 contractions greater than 1.0% are followed by positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample validation.
